monday.objective: Students will analyze character development in narrative texts

anticipatory_set.original_content: Quick write: Think of a character from a book or movie. How did they change from beginning to end? Share with partner.

instructional_input.original_content: Mini-lesson on character development: direct vs. indirect characterization, character arc, evidence from text. Model analysis using short story excerpt. Introduce character analysis graphic organizer.

guided_practice.original_content: Read short story 'The Treasure of Lemon Brown' together. Stop at key points to discuss character traits and changes. Complete graphic organizer as a class for main character.

independent_practice.original_content: Students complete character analysis for secondary character. Find text evidence for at least 3 character traits. Write paragraph explaining character development.

assessment.original_content: Review character analysis paragraphs. Check for text evidence and clear explanation of development. Provide feedback on analysis depth.

homework.original_content: Continue reading assigned novel (chapters 5-7). Complete character tracker for protagonist. Note changes in behavior, thoughts, or relationships.

tuesday.objective: Students will write a character analysis essay using text evidence

anticipatory_set.original_content: Review character development from yesterday. Share one interesting change noticed in homework reading. Discuss: How do authors show character change?

instructional_input.original_content: Model writing character analysis essay: thesis statement, topic sentences, text evidence, analysis. Show how to integrate quotes. Demonstrate RACE strategy (Restate, Answer, Cite, Explain).

guided_practice.original_content: Work together to write introduction paragraph for class essay. Create thesis statement about character development. Outline body paragraphs with main ideas.

independent_practice.original_content: Students write character analysis essay (3 paragraphs minimum). Include introduction with thesis, body paragraph with evidence, conclusion. Use RACE strategy for evidence paragraphs.

assessment.original_content: Formative: Conference with students during writing. Check thesis statements and evidence selection. Provide guidance on analysis vs. summary.

homework.original_content: Complete essay draft. Bring printed copy for peer review tomorrow. Read chapters 8-10 of novel.
